SessionNotes: Set up mini pc with Proxmox, for Project Atlas, troubleshot networking issues related to dedicated ip adressing, troubleshot BIOS for OS install boot not reading on boot order.

Installed and configured NGINX Proxy. Learned reverse proxy fundamentals and how NPM routes requests to internal services. Evaluated reverse proxy options and selected NGINX as longterm solution. Learned difference of recursive DNS vs Local DNS rewrites. Installed and configured Adguard Home for network wide DNS and ad blocking. Configured Quad9 DNS over HTTPs as unpstream resolver. Created reverse proxy hosts for services. Sucessfully validated the complete request path : Client> AdGuard> DNS Rewrite> NGINX> Docker Service. Investigated Windows DNS behavior and identified IPV6 DNS precedence over manual configured IPV4. Confirmed architecture by testing Jellyfin through internal hostname. Intergated Jellyfin and Navidrome among other services into Homarr dashboard. Established the inital internal service architecture for Project Atlas.
Difficulties: IPV6 DNS took precedence over local DNS rewrites, Windows continued querying the ISP's IPv6 DNS instead of AdGuard. Determined issues was client side DNS, Validated functionality by temp disabling IPv6 during testing before restoring it.
NextGoals: Implement operations tasks: automatic health monitoring, back up strategy, proxmox snapshots, disk usuage alerts, SMART mointoring, Automatic container updates (carfeully controlled) Then move on to Security with WireGuard VPN, Fail2Ban (if applicable) Internal only administration, HTTPS where appropriate and Proper documentation.
Technologies: DNS, Docker, Networking, Proxmox, Docker Compose, Adguard Home, NGINX Proxy Manager, Hommar, Jellyfin, Navidrome, Glances, Reverse Proxy, DNS Rewriting, HTTP Networking, Home Lab Architecture

SessionNotes: Verified Docker LXC storage utilization and confirmed backup size requirements. Confirmed the /media bind mount is excluded from Proxmox container backups. Successfully created the first full Proxmox backup of CT100 using Stop mode with ZSTD compression, producing a 3.24 GB compressed recovery archive. Verified the backup completed successfully and is visible within Proxmox for future restoration. Established the first Atlas v1.0 disaster recovery point prior to expanding the homelab infrastructure and reviewed the backup strategy and roadmap for upcoming deployments including Authentik, Uptime Kuma, Grafana, and network-wide AdGuard DNS.
NextGoals: Atlas reached its first stable recovery milestone. Successfully created and verified the initial Proxmox backup before deploying additional infrastructure. This establishes a known-good restore point, allowing future services such as Authentik, Uptime Kuma, and Grafana to be deployed with significantly reduced risk. Next is Authentik, Kuma and Grafana.
Technologies: Backup & Disaster Recovery, Wireguard VPN, NGINX Proxy Manager, Adguard Home, Docker, Proxmox, Linux
Difficulties: Discovered that Proxmox LXC snapshots are not supported due to the /media bind mount configuration, requiring the use of Stop mode backups instead. Verified that standard Proxmox backups exclude the bind-mounted media storage while preserving the complete Docker environment and application configurations. No critical issues or failures were encountered during the backup process, and the backup completed successfully on the first attempt.

SessionNotes: Developed homelab further, established Authentik and verified it's use accross the network for services
